title: Adi Aathaadi Lyrics Kadalora Kavithaigal
stanzas:
Adi aathaadi
Ila manasonnu rekka
Katti parakkudhu sarithaanaa..

Uyirodu uravaadum
Orukodi aanandham
Ivan megam aaga
Yaaro kaaranam.

Adi aathaadi
Ila manasonnu rekka
Katti parakkudhu sarithaanaa..
Adi ammaadi ..

Mela pogum megam ellam
Kattu pattu aadaadho.
Unna paarthu alaigal ellam
Mettu katti paadaadho.

Ippadi naan aanadhilla
Puththi maari ponadhilla
Munna pola nerndhadhilla
Mookku nuni verthadhilla

Kanni ponnu nenjukulla
Kaththi sanda kandaayo
Pada padakkum nenjukulla
Pattaam poochi
Paarthaayo ..
Yesa kaettaayo ..

Dhaaga paatta nenjukulle
Yegapatta sandhosam
Unma sollu ponne enna
Enna seyya uththesam

Vaartha onnu vaai varaikkum
Vandhu vandhu povadhenna
Kattumaram poo pookka
Vaasapattu aavadhenna..

Kattuthari kaala naane
Kannukutti aanene .
Thottu thottu thendral pesa
Thookkam kettu ponene..
Sol ponmaane ..

Adi aathaadi
Ila manasonnu rekka
Katti parakkudhu sarithaanaa..

Uyirodu uravaadum
Orukodi aanandham
Ivan megam aaga
Yaaro kaaranam.

Adi aathaadi
Ila manasonnu rekka
Katti parakkudhu sarithaanaa..
Adi aathaadi ..

enrichedMetadata.faq:
Frequently Asked Questions

Who are the lead actors in the movie Kadalora Kavithaigal?

The lead roles are played by Sathyaraj and Rekha.

What is the theme of the song Adi Aathaadi?

The song beautifully portrays the emotions of love and longing between the lead characters.

Who composed the music for Kadalora Kavithaigal?

The music was composed by the legendary Ilaiyaraaja.

What year was the movie Kadalora Kavithaigal released?

The movie was released in the year 1986.

Who wrote the lyrics for the song Adi Aathaadi?

The lyrics were penned by the renowned poet Vairamuthu.
enrichedMetadata.summary: The song "Adi Aathaadi" from the 1986 Tamil movie "Kadalora Kavithaigal" features the lead actors Sathyaraj and Rekha. This melodious duet, composed by Ilaiyaraaja and penned by Vairamuthu, is sung by S. Janaki and Malaysia Vasudevan. The song captures the tender emotions of love and longing, set against the backdrop of the film's poignant narrative. Its timeless melody and heartfelt lyrics continue to resonate with audiences, making it a cherished classic in Tamil cinema.
enrichedMetadata.high_ctr_intro: Adi Aathaadi, a melodious duet from the movie Kadalora Kavithaigal, captures the essence of love and longing. Composed by the legendary Ilaiyaraaja and penned by the poetic Vairamuthu, this song is beautifully rendered by S. Janaki and Malaysia Vasudevan. Its timeless charm and emotional depth make it a cherished classic in Tamil cinema.
stanzaMeanings: Oh dear girl, the young heart spreads its wings and flies, is it true?
Oh dear mother, a wave comes and strikes the heart, is it so?
A million joys embrace life, someone is the reason for this cloud.
Oh dear girl, the young heart spreads its wings and flies, is it true? Oh dear mother...
Do the clouds that rise above not dance when tied? Do the waves, seeing you, not compose and sing?
I have not become like this, my mind has not changed, I am not as straightforward as before, my nose tip has not turned.
Did you see a knife fight in the heart of a young girl? Did you see a fluttering butterfly in the heart? Did you hear the melody?
A song of thirst in the heart brings immense joy. Tell me the truth, girl, what is your intention?
Why does a word come to the lips and then go away? Why does a raft bloom flowers and become fragrant?
I became a tied thread, I became a calf. The breeze touched and spoke, and I lost my sleep. Speak, golden deer.
Oh dear girl, the young heart spreads its wings and flies, is it true?
Oh dear mother, a wave comes and strikes the heart, is it so?
A million joys embrace life, someone is the reason for this cloud.
Oh dear girl, the young heart spreads its wings and flies, is it true? Oh dear girl...
